cards.note: In French, 'enceint' is the masculine form, 'enceinte' is the feminine form.
Used to describe children traveling alone

cards.fr: Ça pèse lourdement sur sa conscience
cards.en: It weighs heavily on their conscience
cards.note: Use 'pèser sur' to express a burden on someone's mind

cards.fr: Je ne veux pas vendre de l'alcool aux mineurs
cards.en: I don't want to sell alcohol to minors
cards.note: Use 'mineurs' to refer to people under the age of majority

cards.fr: Ça ne peut pas ne pas avoir un impact sur l'esprit de quelqu'un
cards.en: It can't help but affect someone's mind
cards.note: Double negation is often used in French for emphasis, but 'ne pas ne pas' is more formal and less common in spoken language, 'ne pas pouvoir ne pas' is a more natural alternative

cards.fr: Les mineurs ne sont pas autorisés
cards.note: Use 'mineur' for a single minor, 'mineurs' for multiple

cards.fr: Ça pèse lourdement sur l'esprit de quelqu'un
cards.en: It weighs heavily on someone's mind
cards.note: Use 'pèse lourdement' for a stronger emotional impact
Informal response to 'merci', 'mine de rien' is not commonly used in modern French
